content: The intrinsic reward signals from curiosity-driven methods (ICM/RND) can act as a self-supervising signal for pruning and prioritizing episodic memory in self-supervised world models, where only surprising, unexpected state transitions that trigger curiosity are retained for long-term planning, instead of storing every experienced trajectory.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
